verbGlossary.ACTIVATES: {source} triggers a previously identified risk from dormant to active status (e.g., delay log entry activates {target} trigger when the documented cause matches a registered risk condition)
verbGlossary.CHRONICLES: {source} creates a time-stamped narrative record of conditions relevant to {target} category (e.g., daily log header chronicles construction execution risk through day-by-day jobsite documentation)
verbGlossary.CLOSES: {source} confirms resolution of {target} exposure by documenting receipt or completion (e.g., material receipt record closes supply chain risk by confirming delivery arrived on site)
verbGlossary.CONSUMES: {source} event uses up a portion of {target} buffer or reserve (e.g., weather log entry consumes schedule buffer when weather days erode available float)
verbGlossary.DOCUMENTS: {source} provides formal written evidence of conditions or events tied to {target} (e.g., accident log entry documents safety risk by recording incident details, root cause, and corrective actions)
verbGlossary.DRIVES: {source} event directly increases or worsens {target} metric (e.g., safety violation log entry drives incident rate TRIR by adding recordable events to the calculation)
verbGlossary.ERODES: {source} event gradually reduces {target} buffer through repeated occurrences (e.g., delay log entries erode schedule float erosion as cumulative delays consume remaining slack)
verbGlossary.ESCALATES: {source} condition raises the severity or urgency of an existing {target} (e.g., delivery log entry escalates schedule risk when late material arrival threatens critical path activities)
verbGlossary.EVIDENCES: {source} data provides observable proof that {target} condition exists or is materializing (e.g., daily log note evidences construction execution risk through documented rework, crew conflicts, or access issues)
verbGlossary.EXPOSES: {source} observation reveals a previously unidentified or underestimated {target} (e.g., daily log inspection entry exposes construction execution risk by documenting failed work that requires correction)
verbGlossary.INFORMS: {source} data provides input that shapes {target} assessment or scoring decision (e.g., daily log inspection entry informs risk score by providing pass/fail evidence for quality evaluation)
verbGlossary.QUANTIFIES: {source} metric assigns a measurable value to {target} dimension (e.g., delay frequency quantifies schedule risk by counting disruption events per period)
verbGlossary.SIGNALS: {source} metric serves as an early warning indicator that {target} is emerging before it fully materializes (e.g., manpower log entry signals labor risk when headcount drops below planned staffing levels)
verbGlossary.SURFACES: {source} narrative uncovers {target} condition buried in daily operations that might otherwise go undetected (e.g., daily log note surfaces coordination risk when field crew documents trade stacking or sequencing conflicts)
verbGlossary.TRACKS: {source} monitors the ongoing status of {target}-related condition over time (e.g., material shipment record tracks supply chain risk by logging transit status and expected delivery dates)
verbGlossary.TRIGGERS: {source} event initiates {target} response or downstream action (e.g., safety violation triggers mitigation action requiring immediate corrective measures on the jobsite)
